summary: Venom stage decomp pass touching grvenom.c, grvenom.h, and gr/types.h. The main value was replacing fake/alias-based Venom ground variable handling with Venom-specific struct overlays, producing a new 100% match for grVenom_80204428 and a large partial implementation improvement for grVenom_80206874, while the bot also reported a regression in grVenom_802053B0.
what_changed: Extended src/melee/gr/types.h for Venom ground variables: grVenom_GroundVars gained xF0, xF4, xF8, xFC, and x100 fields with gp+offset comments.
Added grVenom_GroundVars2 as an alternate Venom overlay containing HSD_JObj* fields xC4 through xDC and an xE0_state union with byte bitfields b0-b7 plus a 2-bit state view.
Added the new venom2 overlay to the Ground union so Venom code can access the same memory through a stage-specific JObj/bitfield layout instead of borrowing another stage layout.

Updated grVenom_80204428 to store Ground_801C3FA4 results into gp->gv.venom2 JObj pointer fields and clear gp->gv.venom2.xE0_state bits directly; the old fake-match comment and mutecity2 bitfield aliasing were removed.
Added PAD_STACK(8) in grVenom_80204428 and small padding locals in grVenom_80204284, matching-oriented changes reflected by bot improvements.
Reworked grVenom_80204B88 lighting state access to use Ground* plus gp->gv.venom2.xE0_state instead of a local GroundVenom_ForLighting cast struct.
Changed grVenom_80205E84 from Vec2* to Vec3* in both grvenom.c and grvenom.h, and removed Vec2 casts at grVenom_802053B0 call sites.
Adjusted grVenom_802052E0 local ordering and zeroed pos with chained assignment to grVe_804DB740 instead of three explicit 0.0F stores.
Cleaned grVenom_80205AD4 raw gp+0xF0/0xF4/0xF8/0xFC/0x100 writes into named gp->gv.venom.xF0/xF4/xF8/xFC/x100 fields.

Removed the #if 0 around grVenom_80206874 and added an 82.52%-reported partial implementation: initializes Venom state, reads grVe_803E5348 table data, spawns or links another Ground_GObj, applies scale with HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z using grVe_804D6A30 offset 0x34, and initializes xD4/xF0/xF4/xF8/xFC.

In grVenom_802053B0, replaced a handwritten assert/dirty-block rotation sequence with HSD_JObjSetRotationY and switched several state accesses from pointer-punned stores to direct gp->gv.venom.xD4/xD8 field use; the bot reported this function regressed from 95.38% to 82.77%.
smart_moves: The strongest fix was modeling the real Venom overlay instead of keeping a fake match that accessed mutecity2 bitfields at the same offset.
Using grVenom_GroundVars2 made the grVenom_80204428 match both cleaner and verified: decomp.dev reported grVenom_80204428 moving from 99.70% to 100.00%.
The xE0_state union gives both forms of access needed by the diff: individual flag clearing through b0-b6 and lighting-state access through xE0_state_pad.state.
Moving fake local layout knowledge from grvenom.c into types.h makes later Venom functions reusable and less dependent on casts to ad hoc structs.
Offset comments in grVenom_GroundVars and grVenom_GroundVars2 make the overlay relationship auditable for future stage groundvars work.
Updating the grVenom_80205E84 prototype to Vec3* aligned the header with call sites that already used Vec3 stack positions.
The PR accepted partial progress where useful: grVenom_80206874 went from disabled/#if 0 to a bot-reported 82.52% match.
decomp_lessons: When a Ground gv region is used as pointers in one function, floats or counters in another, and bitfields in a third, prefer explicit stage-specific overlays in types.h over fake aliases to another stage struct.
Bitfield layout fixes can turn near-matches into exact matches; here, replacing mutecity2.xC4_flags aliasing with venom2.xE0_state was enough to finish grVenom_80204428.
Do not hide important layout assumptions in local fake structs if the layout belongs to a shared Ground union; promote the layout once several functions need it.
Typed cleanup can affect matching both ways: the PR improved grVenom_80204428 and grVenom_80206874, but decomp.dev reported grVenom_802053B0 regressed after cleanup in that function.
Header signatures should match the real data passed by callers; changing Vec2* to Vec3* removed casts around Vec3 stack positions.
Small stack/register tactics still matter in this codebase: PAD_STACK, explicit padding locals, and local declaration ordering are all visible in this diff.
If a function comment contains a match percentage, verify it after bot results; grVenom_80206874 still had an old 32.92% @todo text while the bot reported 82.52% after the PR.
naming_conventions: Venom stage functions keep address-style names such as grVenom_80204428, grVenom_80204B88, grVenom_802053B0, and grVenom_80206874.
Stage ground variable structs use gr<Stage>_GroundVars naming; the alternate overlay added here is grVenom_GroundVars2 and is exposed as gp->gv.venom2.
Unknown ground fields retain x-offset names such as xC4, xD8, xE0, xF8, and x100, with comments documenting relative and absolute gp offsets.
Venom data symbols retain grVe_* names such as grVe_803E5348, grVe_804D6A30, grVe_804D6A34, and grVe_804DB740.
Bitfield views used exact descriptive names xE0_state and xE0_state_pad, while unnamed flag bits remain b0 through b7.
Ground object and joint object access follows existing GET_GROUND, GET_JOBJ, Ground_GObj, HSD_GObj, and HSD_JObj conventions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in grVenom_80204428 to satisfy stack/codegen requirements.
Inserted u32 pad/pad2 locals in grVenom_80204284 and reordered Vec3 jobj_pos in grVenom_802052E0, indicating stack layout/register-allocation tuning.
Replaced pointer-punned JObj stores with a typed HSD_JObj* overlay so Ground_801C3FA4 assignments compile through normal field access.
Used a union for xE0_state so code can read env_flags as a byte and update a 2-bit state field at the same offset.
Used grVe_804DB740 for zeroing a Vec3 via pos->x = pos->y = pos->z, which also coincided with .sdata2 improvement in the bot report.

grVenom_80206874 uses raw table-pointer arithmetic against grVe_803E5348 with offsets like 0x2C, 0x38, 0x324, and 0x20; future work should preserve those exact access patterns until the table struct is known.
Scale setup in grVenom_80206874 deliberately reloads grVe_804D6A30 and recomputes scale before HSD_JObjSetScaleX, Y, and Z, likely for matching codegen.
The HSD_JObjSetRotationY replacement in grVenom_802053B0 is cleaner than the removed handwritten assert block, but the bot regression means this tactic should be revisited before copying it blindly.
review_feedback: No human PR body, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code 65.03%, a +0.01% / +284 byte overall improvement.
decomp.dev bot reported one new match: main/melee/gr/grvenom grVenom_80204428 from 99.70% to 100.00%.
decomp.dev bot reported improvements for grVenom_80206874 from 0.00% to 82.52%, .sdata2 from 10.17% to 19.53%, and grVenom_80204284 from 97.58% to 97.74%.
decomp.dev bot reported a regression for grVenom_802053B0 from 95.38% to 82.77%.
follow_up_queries: Can grVenom_802053B0 recover the bot-reported 95.38% to 82.77% regression while keeping the cleaner Vec3 signature and Venom groundvar overlay?
Should grVenom_80206874's stale @todo percentage be updated from 32.92% now that decomp.dev reported 82.52%?
Can grVe_804D6A30 be expanded into a fuller attribute struct so raw offset reads at 0x2C, 0x34, and later offsets can become named fields?
Should grVenom_GroundVars xD4 and xD8 remain f32, or should future work add an integer/union view for state-like uses seen in grVenom_802053B0 and grVenom_80206874?
Can grVe_803E5348 table accesses in grVenom_80206874 be replaced with a typed spawn/table struct without losing the current 82.52% match?
Audit other stages for fake bitfield aliasing similar to the removed mutecity2 workaround and replace with stage-specific overlays.
